description: Following Elon Musk's acquisition of Twitter, the platform launched the $8 'Twitter Blue' verification subscription, fundamentally changing the platform's authentication policy. The move faced intense backlash regarding potential impersonation risks and the erosion of journalistic credibility, culminating in a significant decline in public trust for the new subscription model.

key_events.description: Elon Musk announces the $8 subscription model via tweet.
High-profile impersonations (Eli Lilly stock crash) trigger security concerns.
Major advertisers suspend spending due to lack of content moderation controls.
Twitter officially removes legacy blue checkmarks, causing further public outcry.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring how corporate-led policy shifts affect brand perception versus user utility.
